RULING: No argument. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT KENYON CONSTRUCTION, INC'S Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement is granted. G.P. Systems, Inc. has not met its initial burden of proof to show that the settlement was not made in good faith. (City of Grand Terrace v. Superior Court (1987) 192 Cal.App.3d 1251, 1261.) The settlement is within ballpark based on the Kenyon Construction, Inc.'s ("Kenyon") proportionate liability for the construction defects associated with its work on the project. The declaration of Bryce Gray is insufficient to substantiate that Kenyon should have allocated more to Cross-Complainants defense costs. Kenyon's objections are sustained. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Sep-26-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
CROSS COMPLAINANTS SPNS GOLDEN GATE, LLC AND STANDARD PACIFIC CORP.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied. First, the Court sustains the objection to the declaration of Mr. Billy. Mr. Billy fails to authenticate the documents attached to his declaration. Second, the Court determines that the trial judge should rule on the duty to defend issue. Movants are not prejudiced because Zurich Insurance and Steadfast are currently defending them. In addition, denying the motion will avoid the practical difficulties of sorting out multiple, and potentially conflicting, duties to assume the active defense of litigation in progress. See Crawford v. Weather Shield Mfg. Inc. (2008) 44 Cal.4th 541, 565, fn.12. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
